codeReview.summary: The copy pass improved many dashboard strings, but it still left two user-facing truthfulness gaps that contradict the feature scope: the older-history failure path still surfaces raw backend error strings when an Error is thrown, and the details panel still exposes implementation-facing cache-source labels in the polished UI.

issues.description: handleLoadMore only uses the new polished fallback string for non-Error throwables. When the backend throws a normal Error, the UI still renders the raw message verbatim via setError(err.message), so users can still see unpolished/generic older-history failure copy instead of the explicit Leagueback wording required by the feature.

The match-details header still renders cache implementation labels (Cached match data, Legacy cached match data) to end users. The feature asked for polished, consistent product copy across details panels; exposing storage-source jargon is not a user-facing product concept and leaves the panel inconsistent with the rest of the web copy pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The web-feature-worker skill says UI features should use browser verification whenever available, or otherwise capture explicit dev-server evidence for fallback validation. This worker only reported source review as its interactive check for a user-facing copy feature, and no shared guidance currently clarifies whether copy-only polish work may skip runtime evidence entirely.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff interactiveChecks only says the worker reviewed updated dashboard source copy; transcript skeleton for d87ca4c-d7e3-4aa2-9d0d-654317e63108 shows tests/typecheck but no browser/dev-server validation step.

summary: Fail: the feature improved many dashboard strings, but it did not fully satisfy the polish contract because older-history errors can still show raw backend text and the details panel still exposes implementation-oriented cache-source labels.
